questions.question_text_full:
Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.
The driver said to the passerby, “Do you know the way to the market?”

options.text: The driver asked the passerby did he know the way to the market.
The driver asked the passerby if he knows the way to the market.
The driver asked the passerby if he knew the way to the market.
The driver asked the passerby whether you know the way to the market.

questions.correct_option_text: The driver asked the passerby if he knew the way to the market.

questions.question_text_full:
Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.
RELUCTANT

Select the correct active form of the given sentence.
He was given another chance by his employer.

options.text: His employer is giving him another chance.
His employer gave him another chance.
His employer has given him another chance.
He gave his employer another chance.

questions.correct_option_text: His employer gave him another chance.

questions.question_text_full:
Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the option that gives their correct order.
A. Far below he saw green meadows and in their midst a village.
B. He sat down and rested in the shadow of a rock.
C. Nunez was in a pass between the mountains.
D. He slowly climbed down the precipices and about midday came to the plain, stiff and tired out.
